inventory.name: golden-path.sh
inventory.description: Entry point / function library sourced (never executed) by all 5 golden commands (/loa /plan /build /review /ship) to detect workflow state, format the journey bar, and resolve golden-to-truename commands.
inventory.help_probe: no --help handling: `bash .claude/scripts/golden-path.sh --help` / `-h` / `help` / no-args ALL empirically return exit 0 with ZERO stdout/stderr output (verified live). File (.claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:1-1021) has no arg-parsing, no `case "$1"` dispatcher, no `"$@"` at all — it only defines `golden_*` functions after running init side-effects. Header comment (line 8-9) states 'Usage: source .claude/scripts/golden-path.sh' — designed to be sourced, not executed.
inventory.json_mode: partial: 2 of ~19 functions (golden_menu_options, golden_trajectory) accept a --json arg; the script itself has no top-level CLI so there is no script-level --json
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Init-time error goes to stderr (line 42). Zero tput/\033/ANSI color codes anywhere (grep confirms 0 hits) — safe for piping. Direct execution produces no stdout/stderr at all (confirmed empirically) — an agent probing this like any other script sees 'exit 0, no output' and cannot tell success from no-op from a silently-swallowed usage screen.
inventory.exit_code_notes: No documented exit-code dictionary for direct execution. File ends on a function definition (line 967-1021) so bash reports exit 0 regardless of args passed. The only intentional non-zero direct-exec path is the grimoire-dir guard at line 40-44: exit 1 if grimoire dir can't be resolved, exit 0 otherwise.
error_samples: [golden-path] ERROR: grimoire dir unresolved (path-lib init failed) — refusing phase detection against root-anchored paths (.claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:42)
evidence: empirical probe: `timeout 10 bash .claude/scripts/golden-path.sh --help/-h/help/<noargs>` → exit 0, 0 lines stdout, 0 lines stderr in all 4 cases
worst_suspects: self_documentation: No --help/-h/help output whatsoever; the only usage doc is a source-code comment (line 8-9) an agent must Read, not something discoverable by probing the CLI.
agent_intuitiveness: Looks like an executable script (shebang, .sh) but silently no-ops on every invocation shape an agent would naturally try, with no signal that 'source it instead' is required.

inventory.name: golden_detect_workflow_state
inventory.description: Single-string 9-state workflow detector (bug_active/initial/prd_created/sdd_created/implementing/reviewing/auditing/complete/sprint_planned) that /loa uses to decide what to show the user; central function of the whole library.
inventory.help_probe: probe skipped: not a standalone CLI target — must be sourced then called as a shell function; no --help on the function itself (source read confirms .claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:467-521 has zero arg parsing).
inventory.json_mode: no — echoes one bare word to stdout (e.g. 'implementing'), no JSON envelope, no schema/version field
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Pure stdout, single line, no stderr on the success path. Suppresses sub-call errors with `>/dev/null 2>&1` (lines 469, 490) so a failure deep inside golden_check_ship_ready or golden_detect_active_bug is invisible.
inventory.exit_code_notes: Always echoes something and returns 0 (every branch ends with `return` after an `echo`); no failure exit path exists, so callers can't distinguish 'cleanly detected' from 'detection degraded by a swallowed jq/stat error upstream'.
worst_suspects: error_pedagogy: All upstream errors (jq parse failures, missing state.json, stat failures) are swallowed via >/dev/null 2>&1 before reaching this function — a mis-detected state gives zero diagnostic trail.
composability: Only callable after sourcing the whole 1021-line file into the current shell; cannot be invoked as an isolated command or piped to directly.

inventory.name: golden_suggest_command
inventory.description: Suggests the single next golden command (/plan, /build, /review, /ship) based on current state; used to tell the user 'run X next'.
inventory.help_probe: probe skipped: function-only, no CLI; per source (.claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:647-678) there is no help output path.
inventory.json_mode: no — bare string like '/build' on stdout
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: stdout only, one line, no stderr
inventory.exit_code_notes: Always returns 0; no way to signal 'ambiguous state, cannot suggest'
worst_suspects: intent_inference: Collapses 9 detailed workflow states down to 4 possible suggestions with no confidence signal or reasoning trace — an agent can't tell if the suggestion is a strong or a weak/fallback inference.

inventory.name: golden_resolve_truename
inventory.description: Maps a golden command (plan/build/review/ship) + optional override arg to the truename command string to actually invoke (e.g. '/implement sprint-3'); the real dispatch logic behind /build etc.
inventory.help_probe: probe skipped: function-only; source at .claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:967-1021 shows no --help branch.
inventory.json_mode: no — plain command string on stdout, or empty string, or an error string on stderr
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Success prints a command string to stdout (e.g. '/implement sprint-2'); some legitimate states print an empty string to stdout (e.g. 'plan' when phase=complete, line 987); validation failure prints to stderr (line 974) and returns 1. Empty-stdout-on-legitimate-no-op and empty-stdout-preceding-an-unrelated-condition are indistinguishable without also checking $?.
inventory.exit_code_notes: Returns 1 only on invalid sprint-ID override (line 976); every other path returns 0 even when it produces empty output (e.g. build with no active sprint falls through with nothing echoed, lines 1004-1008) — success/no-op and validation-failure are the only two distinct codes.
error_samples: Invalid sprint ID: ${override} (expected: sprint-N) (.claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:974)
worst_suspects: output_parseability: Legitimate 'nothing to do' (empty stdout, exit 0) is byte-identical to some silent-fallthrough branches (e.g. build/review with no target) — callers must add their own emptiness checks since the function gives no structured signal.

inventory.name: golden_check_ship_ready
inventory.description: Gate check for /ship: walks all sprints, returns 0 if every sprint is complete-or-reviewed-and-audited, else 1 with a plain-English reason on stdout.
inventory.help_probe: probe skipped: function-only, .claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:219-245.
inventory.json_mode: no
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Reason string printed to stdout ONLY on failure branches (lines 224, 234, 238); on the ready branch nothing is printed at all (line 244 `return 0` with no echo) — presence/absence of stdout is the only success signal.
inventory.exit_code_notes: Boolean only: 0 = ready, 1 = not ready. No distinct codes for 'no sprint plan' vs 'unreviewed' vs 'unaudited' — an agent must string-match the stdout reason to branch programmatically.
error_samples: No sprint plan found. Run /plan first. (.claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:224)
${sprint_id} has not been reviewed. Run /review first. (.claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:234)
${sprint_id} has not been audited. Run /review first. (.claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:238)
worst_suspects: output_parseability: 3 distinct failure reasons are only distinguishable by regex/string-match on free text, not by exit code or a machine field — this go/no-go gate has no --json mode.

inventory.help_probe: probe skipped: function-only, .claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:155-175.

inventory.name: golden_detect_active_bug
inventory.description: Finds the most-recently-modified non-terminal (.run/bugs/*/state.json) bug and returns 'bug_id:md5hash' for TOCTOU-safe consumption; overrides all other workflow-state priority when present.
inventory.help_probe: probe skipped: function-only, .claude/scripts/golden-path.sh:691-720.
inventory.json_mode: no — custom 'bug_id:hash' colon-delimited scalar, not JSON

inventory.name: 0/1 boolean overload
inventory.description: Library-wide convention: every golden_* function returns 0 for 'found/ready/valid' and 1 for 'not found/not ready/invalid', reused across ~15 semantically different functions with no distinct codes.
inventory.help_probe: probe skipped: not a runnable surface, a cross-cutting convention observed across the whole file.

worst_suspects: determinism_and_reproducibility: Fully deterministic per-function, but the reused 0/1 pair across ~15 functions means composing them (e.g. piping one's exit code into another's conditional) requires per-function tribal knowledge with no library-level dictionary to consult.

inventory.name: beads-health.sh
inventory.description: Top-level comprehensive health check for beads_rust infra; per CLAUDE.md this is invoked at every workflow boundary ('.claude/scripts/beads/beads-health.sh --json') — the primary 'is beads usable right now' gate agents call before task tracking ops.
inventory.help_probe: no --help handling: falls into the catch-all unknown-option branch. Ran `timeout 10 bash beads-health.sh --help` -> stdout/stderr: 'Unknown option: --help' (stderr), EXIT:1.
inventory.json_mode: yes: --json (mutually exclusive with default text mode; both share the same check pipeline)
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: All output (JSON and text) goes to stdout via output_json/output_text; no ANSI/tput usage anywhere in the file (grep-confirmed). But --repair's dispatched sub-tool output is redirected unconditionally to stderr ('>&2' at line 531) even in --json mode, so repair diagnostics never appear in the JSON payload an agent parses.
inventory.exit_code_notes: Documented 6-code dictionary in header (0 HEALTHY,1 NOT_INSTALLED,2 NOT_INITIALIZED,3 MIGRATION_NEEDED,4 DEGRADED,5 UNHEALTHY), mirrored in output. determine_status() encodes status via its OWN return code under 'set -e', requiring an explicit set +e/set -e bracket (lines 572-575) to avoid losing all output entirely — the fragility is flagged in the script's own comment citing bug #228.
error_samples: "Unknown option: $1" (beads-health.sh:104)
evidence: beads-health.sh:566-575 (set +e/set -e bracket around determine_status, re bug #228)
worst_suspects: self_documentation: fully documented usage exists only in a header comment (lines 14-15), never surfaced at runtime via --help — an agent has no in-band way to discover the flag set.

inventory.name: --json
inventory.description: Switches output from human text to a single-line JSON object with status/checks/recommendations — the mode agents use for programmatic health gating.
inventory.help_probe: n/a (covered by the script-level --help probe above; --json itself was not separately probed since it requires the safe read-only default pipeline, which the family constraint restricts to only help-form invocations).
inventory.json_mode: yes: --json is the surface itself
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: JSON emitted to stdout via a single `printf '%s' "${_out}"` (line 421) with no trailing newline; recommendations array built with `jq -nR '[inputs]'` (line 393) which is properly escaped (no string-interpolation injection risk here, unlike several sibling scripts).
inventory.exit_code_notes: Exit code is embedded BOTH in the process exit status and duplicated inside the JSON body's "exit_code" field (line 401) — two channels an agent could read that could in principle diverge if output_json is ever called with a status/code pair that doesn't match determine_status()'s own return.
evidence: beads-health.sh:398-420 (JSON body includes duplicate exit_code field)
worst_suspects: determinism_and_reproducibility: exit_code appears in two places (process exit + JSON field) that are set from the same variable in the happy path but are two independent write sites (lines 401 and 584) an agent must trust stay in sync.

inventory.name: --verbose
inventory.description: Expands text-mode output with a per-check breakdown (binary/initialized/database/schema/doctor/jsonl); has no effect at all in --json mode.
inventory.help_probe: n/a (covered by script-level --help probe).
inventory.json_mode: no: --verbose is silently ignored when combined with --json (VERBOSE variable is never read inside output_json, only output_text) — passing both flags together gives no indication that --verbose was a no-op.
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Verbose block printed to stdout inline with the rest of output_text (lines 435-444); no separation from the primary status line.
inventory.exit_code_notes: No effect on exit code.
worst_suspects: intent_inference: `beads-health.sh --json --verbose` silently drops --verbose with zero warning — an agent combining flags reasonably (more detail + machine format) gets less than expected with no signal.

inventory.name: --quick
inventory.description: Skips the (potentially slow) `br doctor` check, marking that check 'skipped' instead of running it — used when agents want a fast pass/fail without the full diagnostic.
inventory.help_probe: n/a (covered by script-level --help probe).
inventory.json_mode: yes: reflected in checks.doctor = "skipped" in JSON output
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: No stream-routing change; same as base script.
inventory.exit_code_notes: Because check_doctor() is skipped, the DEGRADED condition it can trigger (checks[doctor]==issues_found -> degraded=true, line 366-368) can never fire under --quick — meaning `--quick` can turn a DEGRADED result into HEALTHY purely by not looking, without any note in the output that a doctor-detectable issue may still exist.
worst_suspects: safety_with_recovery: --quick can silently upgrade a real DEGRADED status to HEALTHY (by skipping the one check that would have caught it) with no annotation that the result is incomplete — an agent gating on --quick output could proceed on false confidence.

inventory.name: --repair
inventory.description: Dispatches to tools/beads-migration-repair.sh when the dirty_issues.marked_at migration bug (issue #661) is detected, then re-runs the check; --dry-run/--force are pass-through flags scoped to this repair path.
inventory.help_probe: n/a (covered by script-level --help probe; not independently probed since --repair changes control flow before argument-only validation and could invoke the external repair tool).
inventory.json_mode: partial: repair-tool output is captured but routed to stderr unconditionally (line 531, '>&2') even under --json, so a caller parsing only stdout as JSON never sees what the repair actually did — only a generic recommendation string if the tool exits non-zero (line 536).
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Repair sub-tool stdout+stderr both redirected to the parent's stderr; the final health JSON/text still goes to stdout as normal — but this means '--repair --json' output is not self-contained (repair evidence is off in a separate stream a caller must also capture).
inventory.exit_code_notes: The repair tool's own exit code is captured (line 532) but never propagated as the beads-health.sh exit code — only used to add a recommendation string (line 536) if non-zero; the final exit code still comes from post-repair determine_status(), so a repair that itself failed can still be masked if the migration check happens to pass anyway.
error_samples: "--repair requested but tools/beads-migration-repair.sh not found at ${repair_tool}" (beads-health.sh:561)
"Repair tool exited ${repair_exit}; see stderr for details." (beads-health.sh:536)
evidence: beads-health.sh:531 (sub-tool output forced to stderr even in --json mode)
worst_suspects: composability: --repair's own success/failure is not reflected in the script's exit code or JSON body — only in a free-text recommendation and stderr noise — making it unsafe to script 'run --repair, check $? for whether repair worked'.

inventory.name: LOA_BEADS_DIR
inventory.description: Overrides the .beads directory location the entire health check operates against; used by test isolation and configurable-paths support per CLAUDE.loa.md.
inventory.help_probe: n/a (env var, not flag-probed).
inventory.json_mode: yes: reflected verbatim as top-level "beads_dir" field in JSON output.
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: No special routing.
inventory.exit_code_notes: n/a
evidence: beads-health.sh:53-58 (comment explaining this override was previously silently ignored when the config-paths helper wasn't present — fixed for cycle-105 sprint-1 T1.5)
worst_suspects: regression_resistance: the inline comment itself documents a prior regression class (override silently ignored when a sourced helper file was missing) — the same silent-fallback risk exists for any future helper-file rename/move since the guard is a simple `[[ -f ... ]]` existence check, not a hard failure.

inventory.name: LOA_BEADS_JSONL_WARN_MB / LOA_BEADS_DB_WARN_MB / LOA_BEADS_SYNC_STALE_HOURS
inventory.description: Three threshold overrides controlling when the JSONL/DB are flagged 'large' and when JSONL is flagged 'stale' (age-based) — directly governs whether DEGRADED fires.
inventory.help_probe: n/a (env vars, not flag-probed).
inventory.json_mode: yes: resulting computed values (db_size_mb, jsonl_size_mb, jsonl_age_hours) appear in JSON checks object, but the THRESHOLDS themselves are not echoed anywhere in the output — an agent reading a DEGRADED JSON result cannot tell from the output alone whether the default 24h staleness window or a custom override was in effect.
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: No special routing.
inventory.exit_code_notes: SYNC_STALE_HOURS directly gates one of the four conditions determine_status() checks for DEGRADED (line 362-364); a caller that doesn't know the active threshold cannot predict when age-based flapping will occur.
evidence: beads-health.sh:398-420 (JSON body omits the threshold values themselves)
worst_suspects: self_documentation: active threshold values that directly determine a DEGRADED verdict are never included in the machine-readable output an agent is expected to act on — they exist only as shell env state outside the script's own report.

inventory.name: 0-5 status contract
inventory.description: The full status/exit-code space (HEALTHY=0, NOT_INSTALLED=1, NOT_INITIALIZED=2, MIGRATION_NEEDED=3, DEGRADED=4, UNHEALTHY=5) that every downstream skill/hook branches on.
inventory.help_probe: n/a.
inventory.json_mode: yes: status string AND exit_code integer both present in JSON body (redundant channels, see --json entry above).
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: n/a.
inventory.exit_code_notes: Priority order is fixed and documented in-code (line 324: 'NOT_INSTALLED > NOT_INITIALIZED > MIGRATION_NEEDED > UNHEALTHY > DEGRADED > HEALTHY') — but the comment's own ordering ('UNHEALTHY > DEGRADED') doesn't match the numeric severity implied by the codes (4 < 5), i.e. code value is NOT priority order (DEGRADED=4 is checked AFTER UNHEALTHY=5 despite having the lower number), which is a documented-but-counterintuitive trap for anyone inferring priority from the numbers alone.
evidence: beads-health.sh:324 (explicit priority-order comment that inverts the numeric ordering)
worst_suspects: agent_intuitiveness: exit codes 4 (DEGRADED) and 5 (UNHEALTHY) look ordinally worse-as-you-go, but the actual check order evaluates UNHEALTHY conditions BEFORE DEGRADED ones — an agent that assumes 'higher code number = checked first / more severe = wins ties' will mismodel the precedence.

inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: n/a.

inventory.name: --opt-out-check
inventory.description: The action agents/hooks use to gate 'is it currently OK to proceed without beads' — returns one of NO_OPT_OUT / OPT_OUT_VALID|<expiry>|<reason> / OPT_OUT_EXPIRED / OPT_OUT_INVALID, each carrying different operational meaning under overlapping exit codes.
inventory.help_probe: n/a — internal subcommand of update-beads-state.sh, covered under the parent script's --help probe above; documented separately here because it has the most exit-code/stdout coupling risk in the family.
inventory.json_mode: no: `OPT_OUT_VALID|${expires_at}|${reason}` (line 259) is a pipe-delimited string an agent must split itself — every other action in this script either prints plain prose or supports `jq .` via --show, but this one decision-critical action invented an ad hoc delimited format instead.
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: All four possible outputs go to stdout regardless of whether they represent a normal state (NO_OPT_OUT, exit 3) or a genuinely invalid one (OPT_OUT_INVALID, also exit 3) — disambiguation requires string comparison on stdout content, not the exit code.
inventory.exit_code_notes: Exit 3 is heavily overloaded: NO_OPT_OUT (line 223, nothing recorded — expected/healthy), OPT_OUT_INVALID (line 230, corrupt state — a real bug), and OPT_OUT_EXPIRED (line 254, recorded-but-stale, plus a side-effect state write clearing opt_out.active at lines 249-251) all return exit 3, while only OPT_OUT_VALID returns 0 (line 260). A caller branching purely on exit code cannot tell 'clear to proceed because nothing was ever opted out' from 'clear to proceed because the record file is corrupt.'
error_samples: "NO_OPT_OUT" (update-beads-state.sh:222, exit 3)
"OPT_OUT_INVALID" (update-beads-state.sh:230, exit 3 — same code as NO_OPT_OUT despite meaning something operationally different)
"OPT_OUT_EXPIRED" (update-beads-state.sh:253, exit 3, plus a side-effect state write clearing opt_out.active)
evidence: update-beads-state.sh:228-231 (OPT_OUT_INVALID branch, same exit code as NO_OPT_OUT)
worst_suspects: output_parseability: three operationally distinct outcomes (never-opted-out / corrupt-record / expired-record) share exit code 3, forcing any caller to string-match stdout rather than branch on the exit status the header comment advertises as the contract.

inventory.name: grimoire-index.sh (default write mode)
inventory.description: Regenerates the unified cross-family knowledge index (KF/vision/lore/handoff/obs) into grimoires/loa/INDEX.md (human) and .run/grimoire-index.json (machine); default invocation with no args. CLAUDE.md's Context Intake Discipline names this exact command as the pre-work KF lookup step.
inventory.help_probe: Live probe: --help and -h both exit 0, print the file's header block comment (lines 2-30, 24 lines) to stdout via `sed -n '2,30p' ... | sed 's/^# {0,1}//'`. Confirmed both flags identical output.
inventory.json_mode: partial: --json mode prints JSON to stdout; default write mode writes JSON+MD to disk and only logs a stderr status line (grimoire-index.sh:250) -- default invocation itself has no stdout payload.
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: log() (line 41) explicitly routes to stderr; grep for tput/\033/ANSI across the file is clean, so piping is 8-bit-clean. Default write mode's only observable stdout is none -- success is a file side-effect plus one stderr line.
inventory.exit_code_notes: No single documented dictionary in --help text. From source: 0 success; 2 unknown CLI arg (line 49); 3 jq missing (line 40); 1 on --validate integrity/drift failure (lines 220,232) or --write malformed-record refusal (line 246).

inventory.name: --validate
inventory.description: CI/pre-commit-style integrity check: confirms every record has non-empty id/path AND that the generated kf-family row count exactly equals the number of '## KF-NNN:' headings in known-failures.md (fail-on-drift), emitting GitHub-Actions-style ::error::/::warning:: annotations.
inventory.help_probe: not probed directly; confirmed via source, MODE="validate" (grimoire-index.sh:46).
inventory.json_mode: no -- stdout is empty on success (or a stderr log line unless --quiet); ::error::/::warning:: always go to stderr.
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: All ::error::/::warning:: lines are explicit `>&2` (lines 220,232,240); the success line is also stderr via log() (line 41) -- --validate never writes anything to stdout.
inventory.exit_code_notes: exit $rc (line 243): rc=0 unless the missing-id/path check OR the KF-heading drift check trips (lines 220, 232) -- both OR into ONE exit code, so a caller cannot distinguish which check failed without parsing stderr text.
error_samples: "::error::[GRIMOIRE-INDEX] $bad entries missing id/path" (grimoire-index.sh:220)
"::error::[GRIMOIRE-INDEX] generated kf Index has ${gen_kf} rows but known-failures.md has ${raw_heads} ## KF- headings — derived Index drifted from source of truth..." (grimoire-index.sh:232)

"::warning::[GRIMOIRE-INDEX] known-failures.md hand ## Index table lists ${hand_rows} KF rows but there are ${raw_heads} ## KF- entries — hand table is stale (informational...)" (grimoire-index.sh:240)
worst_suspects: error_pedagogy: two structurally different failure classes (missing id/path vs KF-heading drift) both collapse to exit 1 -- the agent must parse stderr prose, not the exit code, to know which repair path applies.

inventory.name: --quiet
inventory.description: Suppresses the log() status line on stderr for write/validate success; has no effect on ::error::/::warning:: lines, which are unconditional bare echo, not log().
inventory.help_probe: not probed directly; source: QUIET=1 (line 47), gated inside log() (line 41).
inventory.json_mode: no
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Only suppresses log()-routed stderr lines; ::error::/::warning:: (lines 220,232,240,246) always fire regardless of --quiet.
inventory.exit_code_notes: No effect on exit codes.
worst_suspects: intent_inference: the name --quiet implies full output suppression, but ::error::/::warning:: lines bypass log() entirely -- an agent piping stderr for logs still sees those under --quiet, a partial and surprising quiet.

inventory.name: exit-code-contract

inventory.json_mode: no

inventory.exit_code_notes: 0: success (any mode). 2: unrecognized CLI arg (line 49) -- fires before jq/mode logic runs. 3: jq missing (line 40, checked after parse_args, line 211). 1: two distinct causes -- --validate integrity/drift failure (lines 220/232, ORed into one rc) or default-write malformed-record refusal (line 246), which uses a STRICTER predicate (adds a family-shape regex check, line 245) than --validate's own check (line 219, id/path only).

inventory.name: silent-skip-on-malformed-input (no error emitted)
inventory.description: emit_obs() silently skips malformed JSONL rows in observations.jsonl (fails jq -e . parse) via a bare `continue`, with NO message at any verbosity level, including without --quiet -- a genuinely silent failure path distinct from the other documented errors.
inventory.help_probe: n/a -- absence-of-error-message finding, not a probed flag.
inventory.json_mode: no
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Nothing is printed for a skipped line -- not even suppressible log() output; this is silent, not merely suppressed.
inventory.exit_code_notes: No exit-code signal either -- the script exits 0 (or whatever --validate/--write's OTHER checks decide) even if every observation line failed to parse.
error_samples: `echo "$line" | jq -e . >/dev/null 2>&1 || continue` (grimoire-index.sh:155) -- the ONLY guard against malformed JSONL, with no companion log/warn call
worst_suspects: error_pedagogy: a corrupted or partially-written observations.jsonl silently loses rows from INDEX.md with zero diagnostic anywhere in the pipeline -- an agent debugging a missing observation has nothing to point at the cause.

inventory.name: kf-write-lib.sh (bare invocation / dispatcher)
inventory.description: Entry point that reads $1 as the subcommand (new|attempt|recur|notes-header) and dispatches to op_*(); bare invocation with zero args prints help text but is treated as a usage error.

Bare (zero-arg) invocation hits the SAME branch but the trailing `[[ "$CMD" == "" ]] && exit 2` fires -- same help text to stdout, exit 2 instead of 0 (not directly probed since bare invocation isn't one of the 3 permitted forms, but unambiguous from source at line 239).
inventory.json_mode: no
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Help/usage text (all 3 forms) goes to stdout via bare sed with no redirect (line 239); log()/die() messages go to stderr (lines 48-49).
inventory.exit_code_notes: Split behavior on ONE branch: --help/-h -> exit 0; bare (no args) -> exit 2, despite IDENTICAL printed text -- an agent cannot distinguish 'help requested' from 'forgot the subcommand' from output alone, only from the exit code.
error_samples: "kf-write: unknown command: $CMD (new|attempt|recur|notes-header)" (kf-write-lib.sh:240, die() prefixes 'kf-write: ')
evidence: live probe: 'help' -> "kf-write: unknown command: help (new|attempt|recur|notes-header)", exit 1
worst_suspects: agent_intuitiveness: the plausible bare word 'help' is NOT recognized as a help request (only '-h'/'--help' work) and falls through to the unknown-command die(), exiting 1 instead of showing usage.

inventory.name: new
inventory.description: Appends a brand-new auto-incremented KF-NNN entry to known-failures.md plus its Index row, in one lock-guarded, verified, atomic operation. Required: --title, --status. Optional fields default to placeholder text ('unspecified', 'none yet', 'not filed', 'none', a literal TODO string for --reading-guide).
inventory.help_probe: probe skipped: 'new' alone (or with --help appended) would attempt a REAL mutation of known-failures.md (op_new acquires the flock and calls verify_and_swap) rather than showing subcommand-scoped help -- not one of the 3 permitted forms, so read from source only to avoid corrupting the tracked file.
inventory.json_mode: no -- op_new() echoes only the freshly-minted id string (e.g. 'KF-042') to stdout on success (kf-write-lib.sh:152); there is no --json flag anywhere in this file.
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Success: log() status line to stderr (line 151) PLUS the bare id to stdout (line 152) -- deliberately separable so `id=$(kf-write-lib.sh new ...)` captures just the id. All die() failures go to stderr only.
inventory.exit_code_notes: Every failure path calls die() (line 49: stderr + exit 1) -- 'title required' vs 'duplicate title' vs 'index table not found' all collapse to the same exit 1.
error_samples: "new: --title required" (kf-write-lib.sh:104)
"new: --status required (empty Status is a malformed entry per IMP-005)" (kf-write-lib.sh:105)
"new: an entry titled \"$title\" already exists — refusing to duplicate" (kf-write-lib.sh:118)
"new: could not locate the ## Index table" (kf-write-lib.sh:129)
worst_suspects: regression_resistance: the duplicate-title guard (line 117) matches an exact, case-insensitive, regex-escaped title string; trailing-whitespace differences or a synonym title bypass it, silently creating a near-duplicate KF entry in the append-only log.

inventory.name: attempt
inventory.description: Appends one row to an EXISTING KF-NNN entry's '### Attempts' table. Requires --id, --date, --what, --outcome, --evidence -- evidence is the load-bearing field per the append-only anti-tamper design (vision-024).
inventory.help_probe: probe skipped: bare 'attempt' or with a nonexistent --id dies with missing/not-found errors, but a valid --id mutates known-failures.md under flock -- not a permitted probe shape, read from source only.
inventory.json_mode: no
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Success is log()-only to stderr (line 179); no stdout payload at all on success (unlike 'new', which echoes the id).
inventory.exit_code_notes: All failures via die() -> exit 1, including 'id not found' (line 166) and the two structural failures ('no Attempts table', 'could not locate rows') -- a caller cannot tell 'bad id' from 'malformed target file' apart except by message text.
error_samples: "attempt: --id KF-NNN required" (kf-write-lib.sh:159)
"attempt: --evidence is REQUIRED (the load-bearing cell: commit SHA / PR# / run ID)" (kf-write-lib.sh:161)
"attempt: ${id} has no '### Attempts' table — add one manually first" (kf-write-lib.sh:169)
"attempt: could not locate the Attempts table rows under ${id}" (kf-write-lib.sh:174)
worst_suspects: error_pedagogy: 'attempt: ${id} not found' doesn't say which file it searched (default vs --file override) or list nearby valid ids, forcing the agent to grep the target file itself to self-correct.

inventory.name: recur
inventory.description: Increments an existing KF-NNN entry's numeric '**Recurrence count**:' field by 1 (and its Index-table cell if numeric); CLAUDE.md instructs agents to call this when hitting an already-documented failure again.
inventory.help_probe: probe skipped: bare 'recur' dies on missing --id, but a valid --id triggers a real mutation under flock -- not a permitted probe shape.
inventory.json_mode: no
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Success message to stderr only (line 208, e.g. 'kf-write: KF-005 Recurrence count 3 -> 4'); a non-integer recurrence value in the INDEX TABLE degrades to a stderr NOTE rather than failing (line 205).
inventory.exit_code_notes: die() -> exit 1 for missing --id, id-not-found, or a non-numeric Recurrence count in the entry BODY (line 193) -- but a non-numeric Index-table cell is explicitly NOT an error (just a logged NOTE, line 205), an intentional body-vs-index asymmetry.
error_samples: "recur: --id KF-NNN required" (kf-write-lib.sh:186)
"recur: ${id} not found" (kf-write-lib.sh:189)
"recur: ${id} Recurrence count is free-text (\"$cur\") — increment it manually to preserve meaning" (kf-write-lib.sh:193)
worst_suspects: determinism_and_reproducibility: whether the Index-table cell updates alongside the body count depends on whether that cell parses as a bare integer (line 200); a free-text Index cell silently diverges going forward, surfaced only via a stderr NOTE an agent may not relay to the user.

inventory.name: notes-header
inventory.description: Prepends a dated '## Decision Log — DATE (CYCLE)' section (with optional --note body) to NOTES.md right after its first '# ' title line. Requires --date and --cycle.
inventory.help_probe: probe skipped: would mutate NOTES.md if a real target exists; not a permitted probe shape.
inventory.json_mode: no
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Success log to stderr only (line 231); no stdout output.
inventory.exit_code_notes: die() -> exit 1 for missing --date/--cycle, missing target file, or the same data-loss guard used elsewhere (output shorter than input, line 229) -- one uniform code for four distinct causes.
error_samples: "notes-header: --date YYYY-MM-DD required" (kf-write-lib.sh:215)
"notes-header: --cycle required" (kf-write-lib.sh:216)
"notes-header: ABORT — output shorter than input" (kf-write-lib.sh:229)
worst_suspects: self_documentation: unlike 'new' (whose flags are enumerated inline across 3 lines, 27-29), notes-header's flags (--date/--cycle/--note) are documented tersely on one line (32) with no example of the actual output format it produces.

inventory.name: --file
inventory.description: Overrides the default target file (known-failures.md for new/attempt/recur, NOTES.md for notes-header) -- lets an agent point the same lib at a test fixture or alternate log.
inventory.help_probe: not probed directly (needs a subcommand + real args to pair with, which is a mutating call, not a permitted probe shape); documented in the header comment ('Common: --file PATH (override target), --quiet', line 33) and confirmed via TARGET="${A[file]-}" (line 89) plus per-op fallback (e.g. line 101).
inventory.json_mode: no
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: n/a -- no output of its own; only changes which file downstream messages reference.
inventory.exit_code_notes: If --file points at a nonexistent path, every op's own `[[ -f "$f" ]] || die "target not found: $f"` guard fires -> exit 1.
error_samples: "target not found: $f" (kf-write-lib.sh:102, 157, 184, 213 — same message reused across all 4 ops)
worst_suspects: composability: the generic `--*` arg parser (line 85) accepts ANY --key silently -- a typo like '--fiel' instead of '--file' is stored and simply ignored, with no 'unknown flag' error at all in this parsing style.

inventory.name: exit-code-contract
inventory.description: Virtually every failure mode (missing required flag, target not found, id not found, data-loss guard trip, unknown subcommand) funnels through one die() helper that always exits 1 -- differentiation is text-only, and lock-acquisition failure is silently swallowed rather than surfaced as any code.
inventory.help_probe: n/a
inventory.json_mode: no
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: die() (line 49) always writes to stderr; the ONE exception to the blanket-1 contract is bare (no-arg) invocation, special-cased to exit 2 (line 239).
inventory.exit_code_notes: Only two codes exist file-wide: 0 (help shown, or a mutating op completed+verified) and 1 (any die() call), except bare invocation, which is 2. There is no distinct code for e.g. 'lock could not be acquired' (flock failure is swallowed by `|| true`, line 77) or an mv/filesystem failure (would propagate as an uncaught error under set -e, not a die() with a tailored message).
error_samples: die(){ echo "kf-write: $*" >&2; exit 1; } (kf-write-lib.sh:49)
with_lock(){ ... flock 9 2>/dev/null || true; } (kf-write-lib.sh:77) — lock ACQUISITION FAILURE is silently ignored
worst_suspects: safety_with_recovery: `flock 9 2>/dev/null || true` means a failed lock acquisition is invisible -- the op proceeds WITHOUT the lock it just claimed, defeating the concurrency-safety guarantee the file's own header comment advertises.

inventory.name: verify_and_swap data-loss guard (shared by new/attempt/recur)
inventory.description: Shared verification gate used by op_new/op_attempt/op_recur that refuses to swap in a candidate replacement file if it's shorter than the original or drops an existing '## KF-NNN' heading -- the mechanical backstop against the #555 stash-data-loss class this project has previously been burned by.
inventory.help_probe: n/a -- internal function, not directly invocable; exercising it live requires a real mutating call outside the permitted probe set.
inventory.json_mode: no
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Both ABORT messages route through die() to stderr; the temp file is rm -f'd before die() so no partial artifact is left on disk (lines 71, 73).
inventory.exit_code_notes: Both trip exit 1 via die() -- the SAME code as every other failure in the file, so a caller cannot distinguish 'ordinary validation error' from 'the data-loss guard just saved you from a corrupt write' by exit code alone.
error_samples: "ABORT — output ($nl lines) shorter than input ($ol); refusing to write (data-loss guard)" (kf-write-lib.sh:71)
"ABORT — would drop existing entries: $(echo $lost | tr '\n' ' ')" (kf-write-lib.sh:73)
worst_suspects: error_pedagogy: when this guard trips it is the single most consequential failure in the file (an averted silent-corruption write), yet it looks byte-for-byte like an ordinary validation die() message -- nothing marks it as 'this saved your data', inviting a naive retry instead of investigation.

inventory.name: memory-query.sh (default: no args -> --index mode)
inventory.description: With zero arguments, defaults to mode=index (~50-tokens-per-entry listing of the last 10 non-private observations) -- the lightest-weight 'has this been observed before' check per CLAUDE.md's Persistent Memory section.
inventory.help_probe: Live probe: --help/-h print the usage() heredoc (lines 47-79) to stdout, exit 0. Live probe: bare 'help' (no dash) is NOT treated as a flag -- falls through the `*)` catch-all (line 422) and becomes the free-text SEARCH QUERY 'help'; check_observations_file() (line 85) then fired first and exited 1 to stderr since observations.jsonl didn't exist at the resolved path on this checkout.
inventory.json_mode: yes: --json is the default output_format (line 337) -- unlike the other 3 scripts in this family, the bare no-args invocation is JSON-on-stdout by default rather than a side-effecting write.
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Result payload always via `echo "$result"` to stdout (line 480); jq failures inside show_index() etc. are swallowed by `2>/dev/null || echo "[]"` (line 112) -- a jq crash and a genuinely empty result are indistinguishable to the caller.
inventory.exit_code_notes: Header comment (lines 14-16) claims '0 - Success, 1 - Invalid arguments or no results' but this is imprecise: an empty result set from show_index() still exits 0 (echoes '[]'); 'no results' as a distinct code doesn't correspond to any path that isn't also just file-not-found or bad-id.
error_samples: "No observations found at $OBSERVATIONS_FILE" / "Memory system may not be initialized." (memory-query.sh:87-88)
"Unknown option: $1" (memory-query.sh:418)
evidence: live probe: 'help' -> "No observations found at /home/merlin/Documents/thj/code/loa/.loa-state/memory/observations.jsonl" + "Memory system may not be initialized.", exit 1
worst_suspects: agent_intuitiveness: bare word 'help' is silently reinterpreted as a search query instead of erroring or showing usage -- a typo'd help invocation yields a confusing 'no observations found' rather than any hint that 'help' wasn't recognized.

inventory.name: --type TYPE
inventory.description: Filters observations to a single .type value (discovery|learning|pattern|error|decision per usage()) at summary-level detail (id/type/timestamp/100-char summary).
inventory.help_probe: not probed directly with a value (requires a 2nd arg beyond the permitted forms); documented in usage() line 54.
inventory.json_mode: yes (default) -- filter_by_type() (lines 150-162) builds its own independent jq pipeline
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: jq failures fall back to '[]' via `2>/dev/null || echo "[]"` (line 161); an invalid --type value is NOT validated against usage()'s own 5-value enum, so a typo silently returns an empty array indistinguishable from 'valid type, zero matches'.
inventory.exit_code_notes: Always exit 0 if execution reaches this far -- even a typo'd --type value (e.g. 'lerning') exits 0 with '[]', not an error.
worst_suspects: error_pedagogy: --type accepts ANY string with zero validation against its own documented enum -- a typo produces a silent empty result rather than an error naming the valid values.

inventory.name: --full ID
inventory.description: Fetches the complete JSON object for one observation by exact .id match -- the 'level 3' / ~500-token disclosure tier.
inventory.help_probe: not probed directly (needs a real ID argument); documented usage() line 58, implemented in show_full() lines 129-147.
inventory.json_mode: yes -- raw jq object, unwrapped (not an array)
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Uses `jq --arg` (line 139) explicitly to avoid injection -- inline comment 'SECURITY FIX M1' (line 137) flags this was a prior vulnerability class.
inventory.exit_code_notes: exit 1 if --full is given with an empty ID (line 134) OR if no matching observation is found (line 143) -- the same code for both distinct causes.
error_samples: "Error: Observation ID required" (memory-query.sh:133)
"Observation not found: $obs_id" (memory-query.sh:142)
worst_suspects: agent_ergonomics: no fuzzy/prefix id matching exists -- an agent must already possess the exact id string from a prior --index/--summary call, making --full only usable as step 2 of a mandatory two-call workflow.

inventory.name: --stats
inventory.description: Emits aggregate counters over all observations: total count, distinct session count, oldest/newest timestamp, and count-by-type breakdown -- a quick corpus health check.
inventory.help_probe: not probed directly (a real read op, not one of the assigned 3 probe forms); documented usage() line 63, implemented show_stats() lines 287-316.
inventory.json_mode: partial: hand-assembled via a `cat <<EOF` heredoc (lines 308-315) that interpolates 4 separately-computed jq results directly into a JSON literal -- NOT run through `jq -n --argjson`, so a value containing a stray double-quote or control byte from an upstream jq result could produce syntactically invalid JSON with no re-validation.
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: show_stats() has its own redundant existence guard (line 288, prints 'No observations recorded.' and returns, no exit) but main() already calls check_observations_file() unconditionally (line 442) before dispatch, making that internal guard effectively dead code on the CLI path.
inventory.exit_code_notes: 0 in all normal cases; check_observations_file() (line 442) still gates on file-missing before stats mode is ever reached, exit 1.
error_samples: "No observations recorded." (memory-query.sh:290) — effectively unreachable via the CLI since check_observations_file already exits first
worst_suspects: regression_resistance: the JSON is built by string-interpolating 4 jq outputs into a bash heredoc rather than one jq -n --argjson pipeline -- a future change to any sub-query introducing an unescaped quote or newline would silently produce invalid JSON with no validator to catch it.

inventory.name: --lore
inventory.description: Undocumented alternate data source: lists lore entries (from .claude/data/lore/discovered/{patterns,visions}.yaml) with lifecycle metadata (references, last_seen, significance, repos) -- entirely separate from the observations.jsonl the rest of the script queries, and requires yq (not just jq).
inventory.help_probe: NOT mentioned anywhere in usage() (lines 47-79) -- an agent running --help would never learn this mode exists; found only by reading source (arg-parse case at lines 397-400).
inventory.json_mode: yes (default) or --table
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: show_lore() (lines 230-284) calls `yq -o=json` per file (line 242) with `2>/dev/null || continue` -- a missing/broken yq silently produces an empty lore listing rather than an error naming the missing dependency.
inventory.exit_code_notes: Bypasses check_observations_file() entirely -- main() special-cases mode==lore (line 431) to return early -- so --lore never fails on a missing observations.jsonl, but also never validates that the lore YAML files exist; a totally absent .claude/data/lore/discovered/ directory just yields an empty array, exit 0.
worst_suspects: self_documentation: --lore, --sort-by, --significance, and --repo are all fully-wired real flags (lines 397-412) that never appear in usage()/-h output -- the only way to discover this entire query mode is reading source.

inventory.name: PROJECT_ROOT / LOA_STATE_DIR (via path-lib.sh get_state_memory_dir) / LORE_DIR / DISCOVERED_DIR
inventory.description: Controls which observations.jsonl / lore YAML files are read. PROJECT_ROOT defaults to $(pwd) (not the script's own directory), so running from a different cwd silently queries a different project's memory. When path-lib.sh's get_state_memory_dir() succeeds, MEMORY_DIR follows whatever LOA_STATE_DIR resolves to (observed live as .loa-state/memory) rather than the grimoires/loa/memory path CLAUDE.md documents and grimoire-index.sh directly reads.
inventory.help_probe: n/a -- not a flag; discovered via a live probe discrepancy (see the default-mode surface: 'help' probe hit .loa-state/memory/observations.jsonl) plus source read.
inventory.json_mode: no
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: No diagnostic names WHICH path was resolved or why (no fallback-chain trace printed) -- an agent seeing 'No observations found at <path>' cannot tell from the message alone whether that's the correct resolved path or a resolution bug.
inventory.exit_code_notes: n/a -- feeds into the default-mode surface's exit_code_notes.
error_samples: "No observations found at /home/merlin/Documents/thj/code/loa/.loa-state/memory/observations.jsonl" (live probe) vs the grimoires/loa/memory/observations.jsonl path referenced throughout CLAUDE.md and read directly by grimoire-index.sh:151
worst_suspects: determinism_and_reproducibility: the same script invoked from two cwds (or with/without path-lib's config-driven LOA_STATE_DIR resolved) reads two entirely different files with no output indication of which config path won -- grimoire-index.sh's sibling reader in the same family reads a DIFFERENT directory than what this run resolved.

inventory.name: unknown-option and file-not-initialized errors
inventory.description: The two most common error surfaces an agent hits: an unrecognized flag, and querying before any observation has ever been written -- plus a third, visually similar but non-error case (empty-but-existing file).
inventory.help_probe: n/a (aggregate finding)
inventory.json_mode: no -- both are plain stderr text even though the script's default output_format is JSON; an agent parsing stdout-as-JSON unconditionally sees nothing on these paths and must rely on the exit code alone.
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: "Unknown option: $1" (line 418) is immediately followed by a full usage() dump on the SAME stderr stream -- the actual error is line 1 of ~34 lines of stderr rather than the most visible line.
inventory.exit_code_notes: "Unknown option" -> exit 1 (line 420); file-not-found -> exit 1 (line 89); empty-but-existing file -> exit 0 with an informational stderr line ('No observations recorded yet.', line 93) -- this third case is NOT an error at all despite looking similar to the other two.
error_samples: "Unknown option: $1" (memory-query.sh:418, immediately followed by usage())
"No observations found at $OBSERVATIONS_FILE" / "Memory system may not be initialized." (memory-query.sh:87-88)
"No observations recorded yet." (memory-query.sh:93) — this one is exit 0, not an error
evidence: memory-query.sh:85-96 (check_observations_file, two exit codes for adjacent-looking conditions)
worst_suspects: error_pedagogy: three visually similar 'nothing here' messages map to two different exit codes (1, 1, 0) with no textual severity marker -- the exit-0 case reads as if it were also a failure.

inventory.name: repo-map-gen.sh (all 3 modes: default write / --json / --validate)
inventory.description: Generates loa's own deterministic lexical PageRank-style symbol map of .claude/ framework code via a Python engine (lib/repo_map.py). Default mode writes grimoires/loa/REPO-MAP.md + .checksum + .run/repo-map.json; --json prints JSON only to stdout (no writes); --validate regenerates to a tmpfile and diffs against the committed REPO-MAP.md. CLAUDE.md names this exact command as the regeneration path.
inventory.help_probe: Live probe: --help and -h both exit 0, print usage() (sed -n '2,32p' on the file's own header comment, 27 lines) to stdout. Live probe: bare 'help' falls to the `*)` catch-all (line 51) -- "repo-map-gen: unknown arg: help" to stderr, exit 2.
inventory.json_mode: yes: --json (gen_json(), line 62, invokes `python3 lib/repo_map.py --emit json`) -- pure stdout, no side-effect writes in this mode.
inventory.stdout_stderr_notes: Default/write mode's only stdout is one final status line ('repo-map: wrote ...', line 93); --validate's success line also goes to stdout (line 83), but its FAILURE path writes both an ::error:: line and a full unified diff to stderr on the same stream with no delimiter (lines 79-80).
inventory.exit_code_notes: 0 success (all 3 modes); 2 unknown CLI arg (line 51); 3 python3 missing (line 58); 1 --validate drift detected (lines 74, 79) OR the committed REPO-MAP.md simply absent (line 73) -- both collapse to the same exit 1, no distinction between 'file missing' and 'file present but stale'.
error_samples: "repo-map-gen: unknown arg: $1" (repo-map-gen.sh:51)
"repo-map-gen: python3 required" (repo-map-gen.sh:58)
"::error::[REPO-MAP-DRIFT] ${OUT_MD} missing -- run: bash .claude/scripts/repo-map-gen.sh" (repo-map-gen.sh:73)
"::error::[REPO-MAP-DRIFT] REPO-MAP.md is stale vs .claude/ code. Regenerate: bash .claude/scripts/repo-map-gen.sh" (repo-map-gen.sh:79)
evidence: live probes: --help/-h exit 0 stdout 27 lines; 'help' -> stderr "repo-map-gen: unknown arg: help", exit 2
worst_suspects: output_parseability: --validate's failure output interleaves a short ::error:: annotation with a full multi-hundred-line unified diff on the same stderr stream with no delimiter -- extracting just the status line requires filtering diff noise first.
